stories.title: Turkey holds up NATO’s €27B fuel pipeline revamp deal
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Turkey has refused to back a €27 billion upgrade of NATO’s military fuel pipeline network, according to two alliance diplomats, as it mounts an eleventh-hour bid to extract concessions on the agreement. Ankara declined to approve the multi-decade deal during a meeting of the alliance’s 32 envoys last week despite countries reaching a provisional compromise this month, unless the Turkish projects in the deal get prioritized funding, the two diplomats said. Both were granted anonymity t

stories.title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
stories.summary: Research during Hungary election shows AI recommended parties not running and gave highly volatile answers to identical prompts AI chatbots provide inaccurate, inconsistent and unreliable guidance to voters asking which party they should back, a study suggests, often recommending the wrong party, not mentioning the right one, or listing parties not even running. “The results raise serious concerns about the reliability of general-purpose AI systems in electoral contexts,” the study , published b

stories.summary: Interviews with residents of Russian-held region point to frustration with Moscow as Ukrainian campaign to isolate it takes toll on daily life First, the guests stopped coming. As Ukrainian drones and missiles pounded Crimea throughout the spring and into the summer, Russian tourists began cancelling their bookings at the small guesthouse Marina runs in Yevpatoria, a resort town on the peninsula’s Black Sea coast. stories.title: Muslim leader speaks out after more than 80 days in ICE detention: ‘It brought back memories from Palestine’

stories.title: French environment minister to resign after passage of controversial farm bill
stories.summary: PARIS — French Minister of Ecological Transition Monique Barbut plans to step down in protest after lawmakers approved controversial farming legislation that exposed major divisions among President Emmanuel Macron’s centrist supporters. A member of the minister’s team, granted anonymity to discuss a decision that has not been shared publicly, said that Barbut told Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu and her own office she would resign after the bill’s expected passage by the French Senate later Tue

stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: Russian warship carries out rare live-fire exercise 45 miles off UK coast
stories.summary: Neustrashimy suddenly announced it intended to fire its guns and conducted 30-minute drill south of Plymouth A Russian warship conducted a rare live-fire exercise in the Channel 45 miles south of Plymouth on Monday, the day Andy Burnham was appointed as prime minister. The Neustrashimy suddenly announced it intended to fire its guns and requested that a British warship, which had been tracking it, retreat to a safe distance. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threat Fast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts. stories.title: Ann Widdecombe was hit on head repeatedly with hammer, court told
stories.summary: Joshua Kerry appears at Westminster magistrates court charged with murder of former MP earlier this month A man has appeared in court charged with the murder of the former MP and Reform UK spokesperson Ann Widdecombe. Joshua Kerry, 28, is accused of killing the 78-year-old former Conservative minister at her home in Haytor, Devon, earlier this month, by hitting her on the head with a hammer 21 times. stories.title: EU farming support contributing to pricier healthy food, says UN
stories.summary: European Union farm subsidies have helped drive up prices of fruit and vegetables, making it harder for people to afford a healthy diet, a United Nations food report finds. stories.title: Hamish Falconer appointed UK’s new Brexit minister
stories.summary: LONDON — Labour MP Hamish Falconer has been appointed as the British government’s new EU relations minister, leading negotiations with the bloc. The new minister takes over from Nick Thomas-Symonds, a close ally of Keir Starmer, who left the government for the backbenches on Monday. Falconer, the MP for Lincoln who was first elected in 2024, has been a junior Foreign Office minister with responsibility for the Middle East since the general election. stories.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens as Gulf coast braces for flooding and surge
stories.summary: Storm warnings stretch from Florida to Louisiana as Bertha brings heavy rain, flooding risk and possible tornadoes Tropical Storm Bertha strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico and was moving slowly toward the Florida Panhandle and Alabama, causing a tropical storm warning and storm surge watches, forecasters said. The storm was about 110 miles (177km) south of Panama City, Florida , and 170 miles south-east of Mobile, Alabama, the Miami-based National Hurricane Center (NHC) said on Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Stand still, make eye contact: Denmark tells tourists how to behave in a wolf encounter
stories.summary: Government urges visitors not to approach or feed wolves as it manages safety amid growing population Danish authorities have advised tourists to stand still, make eye contact and put up “maximum resistance” in the event of attack if they encounter a wolf while on holiday. After a 200-year absence, wolves started returning to Denmark in 2012. There are now estimated to be 49 wolves on the Danish peninsular of Jutland. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fourth person dies in New York City legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: US health officials say source of Upper East Side cluster has likely been eliminated after 76 people fell ill An outbreak of legionnaires’ disease in New York City has claimed a fourth life, officials have said, as they again expressed confidence the source of the exposure had been eliminated. stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. stories.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
stories.summary: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June. The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
